compiled_state.title: As a skill author, my skill evals run automatically when I commit skill changes
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Integrate Tier 1 eval invocation into record-test-status.sh. When staged files match skill paths (plugins/dso/skills/*/), map to the parent skill directory and invoke the orchestrator for that skill's evals as part of the commit-time test gate.

**Why**: Skill regressions should be caught at commit time, not after merge. This closes the gap between "skill file changed" and "skill quality verified."

**Scope**:
- IN: Staged skill file detection (git diff --cached matching plugins/dso/skills/*/), parent skill directory mapping, orchestrator invocation from record-test-status.sh, exit code integration with test-gate-status contract
- OUT: Tier 2 daily CI (separate story), new eval authoring, RED marker support for evals (not applicable — evals are pass/fail, not TDD RED/GREEN)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, committing a change to any skill file automatically triggers that skill's evals via the commit-time test gate, with results reflected in test-gate-status
  ← Satisfies: "record-test-status.sh maps staged skill files to their parent skill directory and invokes the orchestrator"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Architecture] record-test-status.sh uses fuzzy_find_associated_tests and .test-index for test discovery — skill files will not match via these mechanisms. A new code path is needed that detects staged files under plugins/dso/skills/*/ and maps them to the parent skill directory's evals/ config, independent of existing fuzzy-match and .test-index
- [Architecture] record-test-status.sh determines test runner by file extension (*.sh → bash, *.py → pytest). run-skill-evals.sh must conform to the existing exit code contract (0=pass, non-zero=fail, 144=timeout). RED marker support does not apply to evals
- [Performance] Claude Code has a ~73s tool timeout ceiling (exit 144/SIGURG). A single promptfoo eval invoking Claude via Agent SDK may take 30-120s. If invoked as a single "test file" in record-test-status.sh, the entire eval restarts on each SIGURG resume. Consider whether evals should be invoked via test-batched.sh or whether per-skill resume granularity is sufficient
- [Reliability] record-test-status.sh is a safeguard file (CLAUDE.md rule 20) — all existing behavior (SIGURG/resume, RED markers, merge commit filtering) must be preserved
- [Performance] Promptfoo invocation adds commit-time latency — keep it proportional to changed skills only
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
